description: Criminal gangs that dominated Sydney's crime scene in the 1920s, known for using razors as weapons after handguns were criminalized in New South Wales. They were involved in illegal drug distribution, prostitution, and other organized crime activities. Key figures included Tilly Devine and Kate Leigh. The gangs were active primarily from 1927 to 1930.

country_links.evidence_quote: Peru , Bolivia and Colombia were all accessible through transpacific merchant shipping routes
country_links.context: The razor gangs sourced cocaine from Bolivia via transpacific merchant shipping routes.
